title: Building credential chains
text: time don’t build chain — high-level functions use default_credential_chain() automatically: line one entry: name left credential try right. chain attempted top bottom, non-interactive methods (client secret, workload identity, managed identity, Azure CLI) come interactive browser/device-code flows. get token without ever mentioning chain: get_token(), get_request_authorizer(), get_token_provider(), get_credential_auth(), get_credential_provider() take chain = argument defaults default_credential_chain().

title: Building credential chains
text: Use credential_chain() build . Pass named entries; entry either: credential class (e.g. ClientSecretCredential), already-constructed Credential instance. names (client_secret, azure_cli) labels – show chain prints verbose/error output, pick something recognisable. Constructing chain authentication. Credential configuration validated chain entry tried, just first token request:
code: chain <- credential_chain(   client_secret = ClientSecretCredential,   azure_cli     = AzureCLICredential )  chain token <- get_token(   scope = "https://graph.microsoft.com/.default",   chain = chain )

Constructed instances different: used -. Use instance chain entry configuration: Credential objects validate configuration construction, incomplete object fails fast rather token time: Note sensitive values redacted credential prints:
code: prod_secret <- ClientSecretCredential$new(   tenant_id = "11111111-1111-1111-1111-111111111111",   client_id = "app-registration-id",   client_secret = "super-secret-value" )  chain <- credential_chain(   prod_secret = prod_secret,   azure_cli = AzureCLICredential )  chain ClientSecretCredential$new(client_secret = NULL) ClientSecretCredential$new(client_secret = "super-secret-value")

code: my_cli <- AzureCLICredential$new(auto_login = FALSE)  chain <- credential_chain(   configured_cli = my_cli,   fallback       = DeviceCodeCredential )

title: Building credential chains
text: DefaultCredential wraps chain object lazily-resolved provider. chain resolved first use winning credential reused later calls:
code: cred <- DefaultCredential$new(   scope = "https://graph.microsoft.com/.default",   chain = chain )  # First call resolves the chain; later calls reuse the same provider. token <- cred$get_token() resp  <- httr2::req_perform(cred$req_auth(httr2::request(url)))

title: Building credential chains
text: Set chain_verbose option (AZR_CHAIN_VERBOSE environment variable) watch chain walked — entry tried, fail, one finally returns token: every entry fails, get_token() aborts combined report listing named entry error raised, can see whole chain came empty.

title: Declaring Azure Storage datasets
text: azr_dataset() declares one dataset. storage account varies tier — container, path, scheme, format shared across every environment: name must match ^[-z][-z0-9_]*$ can used stable lookup key, path must non-empty (dataset point container root), format must one delta, parquet, csv, tsv, json, avro, orc, text.
code: orders <- azr_dataset(   name      = "orders",   scheme    = "abfss",   container = "raw",   storage   = list(prod = "stprod001", preprod = "stpreprod001"),   path      = "sales/orders",   format    = "delta" )  orders

title: Declaring Azure Storage datasets
text: azr_dataset_uri() turns dataset concrete URI one tier. default uri_type = "hadoop" produces ABFS form used Spark, Flink, Trino, hadoop-azure consumer: Switch tier point dataset different storage account, ask https form: Requesting tier dataset doesn’t declare error lists tiers , misconfiguration fails loudly rather silently reading wrong environment.

title: Declaring Azure Storage datasets
text: tier defaults dataset_tier option, production code rarely needs pass . Set via options(azr.dataset_tier = ...) AZR_DATASET_TIER environment variable (default "prod") — example, pre-production job can export AZR_DATASET_TIER=preprod every azr_dataset_uri() call follows.

title: Declaring Azure Storage datasets
text: URI alone isn’t enough load data — reader also needs format. azr_resolve_dataset() returns azr_dataset_manifest carrying name, uri, format together (call .list() plain R list): exactly shape reader like sparklyr::spark_read_source() wants:
code: m <- azr_resolve_dataset(orders, tier = "prod") m #> <azr_dataset_manifest:orders> #> uri: abfss://raw@stprod001.dfs.core.windows.net/sales/orders #> format: delta m <- azr_resolve_dataset(orders, tier = "prod") spark_read_source(sc, name = m@name, source = m@format, path = m@uri)

what: Creating a dataset from a URI
title: Declaring Azure Storage datasets
text: already full storage URI, azr_dataset_from_uri() parses dataset. parsed storage account bound tier; name defaults last path segment (file extension removed); format inferred URI’s file extension (_delta_log segment) don’t pass : directory URI file extension infer , format defaults "delta". Pass format explicitly override (example directory Parquet files): make dataset multi-tier, supply additional accounts via storage:
code: file <- azr_dataset_from_uri(   "abfss://raw@stprod001.dfs.core.windows.net/sales/orders/part.parquet",   name = "orders_file" )  file #> <azr_dataset:orders_file> #> scheme: abfss #> container: raw #> path: sales/orders/part.parquet #> format: parquet #> storage: prod=stprod001 # Directory URI → format defaults to "delta" azr_dataset_from_uri(   "abfss://raw@stprod001.dfs.core.windows.net/sales/orders",   name = "orders" )  # Override the default for a non-delta directory azr_dataset_from_uri(   "abfss://raw@stprod001.dfs.core.windows.net/sales/orders",   name   = "orders",   format = "parquet" ) azr_dataset_from_uri(   "abfss://raw@stprod001.dfs.core.windows.net/sales/orders",   name    = "orders",   format  = "delta",   tier    = "prod",   storage = list(preprod = "stpreprod001") )

title: Declaring Azure Storage datasets
text: azr_catalog() holds ordered collection datasets unique names. behaves like named container: index [[, use names() length(): azr_dataset_uri() azr_resolve_dataset() dispatch catalog. name, return one entry per dataset, keyed name: Pass name resolve single dataset catalog:
code: catalog <- azr_catalog(datasets = list(orders, file))  names(catalog) #> "orders" "orders_file" length(catalog) #> 2 catalog[["orders"]] azr_dataset_uri(catalog, tier = "prod") #> orders      "abfss://raw@stprod001.dfs.core.windows.net/sales/orders" #> orders_file "abfss://raw@stprod001.dfs.core.windows.net/sales/orders/part.parquet" azr_dataset_uri(catalog, tier = "prod", name = "orders") azr_resolve_dataset(catalog, tier = "prod", name = "orders")

title: Declaring Azure Storage datasets
text: catalog round-trips JSON, can keep dataset definitions version control load runtime. azr_catalog_write() serialises catalog azr_catalog_read() reads back: JSON shape one object per dataset top-level datasets array: azr_catalog_read() validates every entry, reporting dataset (index name) invalid — malformed catalog fails load time rather pipeline later tries read dataset.

title: Declaring Azure Storage datasets
text: typical flow: keep datasets.json repo, read catalog, resolve dataset need current tier. code runs unchanged every environment; dataset_tier option (AZR_DATASET_TIER) decides storage account URIs point .
code: catalog <- azr_catalog_read("datasets.json")  m <- azr_resolve_dataset(catalog, name = "orders")  # tier from AZR_DATASET_TIER df <- spark_read_source(sc, name = m@name, source = m@format, path = m@uri)

text: azr_options() prints every option, resolved value, value came ([default], [envvar], [option]), underlying environment variable: setting options(azr.dataset_tier = "preprod"), call shows new value came : azr_options() also returns information invisibly data.frame, use scripts diagnostics: option held sensitive value, azr_options() redact "<hidden>"; pass mask = FALSE see raw values.

title: Credential Chain for Seamless OAuth 2.0 Authentication to Azure Services
text: package supports creating Credential chains Authentication : Client Secret Credential: Service principal authentication client ID secret Workload Identity Credential: Federated token exchange Kubernetes/AKS workloads Managed Identity Credential: System- user-assigned managed identity via IMDS endpoint Azure CLI Credential: Leverages existing Azure CLI (az) login Authorization Code Flow: Interactive browser-based authentication Device Code Flow: Authentication headless CLI environments interactive development, azr allows browser-based login flows, batch/production mode seamlessly falls back non-interactive methods.

title: Credential Chain for Seamless OAuth 2.0 Authentication to Azure Services
text: simplest way authenticate using get_token(), automatically tries different authentication methods one succeeds: Alternatively, use get_request_authorizer() get function adds authentication requests: can customize authentication methods tried order:
code: library(azr)  # Get a token using the default credential chain token <- get_token(   tenant_id = "your-tenant-id",   scope = "https://management.azure.com/.default" )  # Use the token with httr2 library(httr2) req <- request("https://management.azure.com/subscriptions?api-version=2020-01-01") |>   req_auth_bearer_token(token$access_token)  resp <- req_perform(req) library(azr) library(httr2)  # Get a request authorizer for Microsoft Graph API azr_req_auth <- get_request_authorizer(   tenant_id = "your-tenant-id",   scope = "https://graph.microsoft.com/.default" )  # Use it to authenticate requests resp <- request("https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/me") |>   azr_req_auth() |>   req_perform() # Define a custom credential chain: try a service principal first, then fall # back to the developer's Azure CLI login ('az login') custom_chain <- credential_chain(   client_secret = ClientSecretCredential,   azure_cli     = AzureCLICredential )  # Use the custom chain - scope, tenant_id, client_id, and client_secret are # forwarded to whichever entry ends up succeeding token <- get_token(   scope         = "https://management.azure.com/.default",   tenant_id     = "mycompany-tenant-id",   # the 'Application Id' and secret used in production/batch mode   client_id     = Sys.getenv("APP_CLIENT_ID"),   client_secret = Sys.getenv("APP_CLIENT_SECRET"),   chain         = custom_chain )

what: Azure Storage Spark / Hadoop configuration — azure_spark_storage_conf
title: Azure Storage Spark / Hadoop configuration — azure_spark_storage_conf
text: Builds named list Hadoop fs.azure.* configuration keys required authenticate Apache Spark (ABFS-compatible runtime) Azure Data Lake Storage Gen2. default prefix = "spark.hadoop" returned list ready pass SparkSession.builder.config(), Databricks cluster's Spark config, spark-defaults.conf — Spark forwards spark.hadoop.* keys Hadoop FileSystem-init time. Pass prefix = NULL get raw fs.azure.* form, suitable core-site.xml sparkContext.hadoopConfiguration().set(...) call. Five authentication types supported: "client_secret" Service principal client secret (ClientCredsTokenProvider). "refresh_token" Delegated user identity via refresh token (RefreshTokenBasedTokenProvider). "workload_identity" Kubernetes workload identity via projected service-account token file (WorkloadIdentityTokenProvider). Requires Hadoop 3.4.1+ / 3.5.0+ (HADOOP-18610). Stock Apache Spark 3.5 ships Hadoop 3.3.4, requires Spark 4.x runtime bundles newer Hadoop (Databricks 14.3+ LTS, Synapse 3.4+). "managed_identity" Azure managed identity via IMDS (MsiTokenProvider). Azure VMs, App Service, Functions, Container Instances, AKS pods without workload identity. Pass client_id select user-assigned identity. "shared_key" Storage account key (SharedKey auth type). Requires storage_account account_key; configured globally. Sovereign clouds (Azure US Government, Azure China) supported passing matching authority_host (e.g. "login.microsoftonline.us"). storage endpoint suffix used scope keys specific account derived authority host. Alternatively, pass fully qualified storage_account like "myacct.dfs.core.usgovcloudapi.net" override derivation entirely.

title: Azure Storage Spark / Hadoop configuration — azure_spark_storage_conf
code: azure_spark_storage_conf(   auth_type = c("refresh_token", "client_secret", "workload_identity",     "managed_identity", "shared_key"),   storage_account = NULL,   tenant_id = default_azure_tenant_id(),   client_id = default_azure_client_id(),   client_secret = default_azure_client_secret(),   refresh_token = default_refresh_token(),   account_key = NULL,   token_file = default_federated_token_file(),   authority_host = default_azure_host(),   prefix = "spark.hadoop" )

title: Azure Storage Spark / Hadoop configuration — azure_spark_storage_conf
text: auth_type Authentication type. One "refresh_token" (default), "client_secret", "workload_identity", "managed_identity", "shared_key". storage_account Optional storage account, either short name ("myacct") fully qualified host ("myacct.dfs.core.windows.net"). NULL (default) keys applied globally accounts. short name supplied endpoint suffix derived authority_host. Required auth_type = "shared_key". tenant_id Azure tenant ID. Defaults default_azure_tenant_id(). client_id Azure application (client) ID. Defaults default_azure_client_id(). auth_type = "managed_identity" selects user-assigned identity (omit leave default system-assigned). client_secret Client secret. Required auth_type = "client_secret". Defaults default_azure_client_secret(). refresh_token Refresh token. Required auth_type = "refresh_token". Defaults default_refresh_token(). account_key Storage account access key. Required auth_type = "shared_key". token_file Path federated service-account token file. Used auth_type = "workload_identity". Defaults default_federated_token_file() (AZURE_FEDERATED_TOKEN_FILE). authority_host Azure authority host (without scheme), e.g. "login.microsoftonline.com". Used build OAuth token endpoint client_secret refresh_token, MSI authority workload_identity managed_identity, derive storage endpoint suffix sovereign clouds. Defaults default_azure_host(). prefix Optional prefix prepended every returned key. Defaults "spark.hadoop", prefix Spark uses forward properties Hadoop. Pass NULL get raw fs.azure.* keys (e.g. core-site.xml hadoopConfiguration() call). trailing dot prefix optional.

title: Azure Storage Spark / Hadoop configuration — azure_spark_storage_conf
text: named list class azure_spark_config. default prefix, keys look like spark.hadoop.fs.azure.account.*; prefix = NULL, keys look like fs.azure.account.*. print method redacts sensitive values (account key, client secret, refresh token).

title: Azure Storage Spark / Hadoop configuration — azure_spark_storage_conf
code: # Global client-secret config (applies to all storage accounts) azure_spark_storage_conf(   auth_type = "client_secret",   tenant_id = "my-tenant",   client_id = "my-client",   client_secret = "my-secret" ) #> $spark.hadoop.fs.azure.account.auth.type #> [1] "OAuth" #>  #> $spark.hadoop.fs.azure.account.oauth.provider.type #> [1] "org.apache.hadoop.fs.azurebfs.oauth2.ClientCredsTokenProvider" #>  #> $spark.hadoop.fs.azure.account.oauth2.client.id #> [1] "my-client" #>  #> $spark.hadoop.fs.azure.account.oauth2.client.secret #> [1] "my-secret" #>  #> $spark.hadoop.fs.azure.account.oauth2.client.endpoint #> [1] "https://login.microsoftonline.com/my-tenant/oauth2/v2.0/token" #>  #> attr(,"class") #> [1] "azure_spark_config" "list"                # Scoped to a specific storage account azure_spark_storage_conf(   auth_type = "client_secret",   storage_account = "mystorageaccount",   tenant_id = "my-tenant",   client_id = "my-client",   client_secret = "my-secret" ) #> $spark.hadoop.fs.azure.account.auth.type.mystorageaccount.dfs.core.windows.net #> [1] "OAuth" #>  #> $spark.hadoop.fs.azure.account.oauth.provider.type.mystorageaccount.dfs.core.windows.net #> [1] "org.apache.hadoop.fs.azurebfs.oauth2.ClientCredsTokenProvider" #>  #> $spark.hadoop.fs.azure.account.oauth2.client.id.mystorageaccount.dfs.core.windows.net #> [1] "my-client" #>  #> $spark.hadoop.fs.azure.account.oauth2.client.secret.mystorageaccount.dfs.core.windows.net #> [1] "my-secret" #>  #> $spark.hadoop.fs.azure.account.oauth2.client.endpoint.mystorageaccount.dfs.core.windows.net #> [1] "https://login.microsoftonline.com/my-tenant/oauth2/v2.0/token" #>  #> attr(,"class") #> [1] "azure_spark_config" "list"                # Azure US Government sovereign cloud azure_spark_storage_conf(   auth_type = "client_secret",   storage_account = "mystorageaccount",   tenant_id = "my-tenant",   client_id = "my-client",   client_secret = "my-secret",   authority_host = "login.microsoftonline.us" ) #> $spark.hadoop.fs.azure.account.auth.type.mystorageaccount.dfs.core.usgovcloudapi.net #> [1] "OAuth" #>  #> $spark.hadoop.fs.azure.account.oauth.provider.type.mystorageaccount.dfs.core.usgovcloudapi.net #> [1] "org.apache.hadoop.fs.azurebfs.oauth2.ClientCredsTokenProvider" #>  #> $spark.hadoop.fs.azure.account.oauth2.client.id.mystorageaccount.dfs.core.usgovcloudapi.net #> [1] "my-client" #>  #> $spark.hadoop.fs.azure.account.oauth2.client.secret.mystorageaccount.dfs.core.usgovcloudapi.net #> [1] "my-secret" #>  #> $spark.hadoop.fs.azure.account.oauth2.client.endpoint.mystorageaccount.dfs.core.usgovcloudapi.net #> [1] "https://login.microsoftonline.us/my-tenant/oauth2/v2.0/token" #>  #> attr(,"class") #> [1] "azure_spark_config" "list"                if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Workload identity on AKS (reads env vars automatically) azure_spark_storage_conf(auth_type = "workload_identity")  # Managed identity on an Azure VM (system-assigned) azure_spark_storage_conf(auth_type = "managed_identity") } # }

what: Parse an Azure Storage path — parse_storage_path
title: Parse an Azure Storage path — parse_storage_path
text: Splits Azure Storage URL Hadoop filesystem path constituent parts. Supports common Azure Storage path formats: abfss:// / abfs:// Azure Data Lake Storage Gen2 (DFS endpoint), used Spark / Hadoop. wasbs:// / wasb:// Legacy Azure Blob filesystem scheme, used older Spark / Hadoop integrations. https:// / http:// Standard Azure Blob DFS REST endpoint, optionally SAS token query string. az:// / azure:// Non-standard aliases used Python tools (e.g. adlfs); parsed using container@account.host/path shape abfs://. format field inferred path best-effort basis: "delta" path contains _delta_log; file extension name ("parquet", "csv", "json", "avro", "orc", "text") last path segment recognised extension; "folder" extension; NA unrecognised extensions.

title: Parse an Azure Storage path — parse_storage_path
Parse an Azure Storage path — parse_storage_path
text: path character string containing Azure Storage path parse.

title: Parse an Azure Storage path — parse_storage_path
text: azure_storage_path object (named list) fields: scheme URL scheme, e.g. "abfss", "wasbs", "https". storage_account Storage account name. endpoint Storage endpoint type: "dfs" "blob". endpoint_suffix Host suffix endpoint label, e.g. "core.windows.net" (Azure public), "core.usgovcloudapi.net" (US Government), "core.chinacloudapi.cn" (China), "storage.azure.net" (DNS-zone endpoints). Use distinguish sovereign clouds public cloud. container Container name. Called "filesystem" ADLS Gen2 / ABFS contexts; refer underlying object. path Path within container, without leading /. Empty string URL points container root. format Inferred dataset file format (see ). query Named list query parameters (e.g. parsed SAS token), NULL none. original original input string.

title: Parse an Azure Storage path — parse_storage_path
code: parse_storage_path(   "abfss://mycontainer@myaccount.dfs.core.windows.net/data/sales/2024" ) #> $scheme #> [1] "abfss" #>  #> $storage_account #> [1] "myaccount" #>  #> $endpoint #> [1] "dfs" #>  #> $endpoint_suffix #> [1] "core.windows.net" #>  #> $container #> [1] "mycontainer" #>  #> $path #> [1] "data/sales/2024" #>  #> $format #> [1] "folder" #>  #> $query #> NULL #>  #> $original #> [1] "abfss://mycontainer@myaccount.dfs.core.windows.net/data/sales/2024" #>  #> attr(,"class") #> [1] "azure_storage_path"  parse_storage_path(   "https://myaccount.blob.core.windows.net/mycontainer/data/events.parquet" ) #> $scheme #> [1] "https" #>  #> $storage_account #> [1] "myaccount" #>  #> $endpoint #> [1] "blob" #>  #> $endpoint_suffix #> [1] "core.windows.net" #>  #> $container #> [1] "mycontainer" #>  #> $path #> [1] "data/events.parquet" #>  #> $format #> [1] "parquet" #>  #> $query #> NULL #>  #> $original #> [1] "https://myaccount.blob.core.windows.net/mycontainer/data/events.parquet" #>  #> attr(,"class") #> [1] "azure_storage_path"  parse_storage_path(   "wasbs://mycontainer@myaccount.blob.core.windows.net/data/delta_table" ) #> $scheme #> [1] "wasbs" #>  #> $storage_account #> [1] "myaccount" #>  #> $endpoint #> [1] "blob" #>  #> $endpoint_suffix #> [1] "core.windows.net" #>  #> $container #> [1] "mycontainer" #>  #> $path #> [1] "data/delta_table" #>  #> $format #> [1] "folder" #>  #> $query #> NULL #>  #> $original #> [1] "wasbs://mycontainer@myaccount.blob.core.windows.net/data/delta_table" #>  #> attr(,"class") #> [1] "azure_storage_path"

title: Write an httr2 Token to the MSAL Token Cache — write_msal_token
text: function adds overwrites AccessToken, RefreshToken (token carries refresh token), Account, AppMetadata sections. Existing entries accounts clients preserved. home_account_id follows MSAL convention "<object_id>.<tenant_id>" object_id Azure AD OID authenticated principal. Cache entry keys built format used Azure CLI MSAL Python: AccessToken: <home_account_id>-<environment>-accesstoken-<client_id>-<realm>-<target> RefreshToken: <home_account_id>-<environment>-refreshtoken-<client_id>-- Account: <home_account_id>-<environment>-<realm> AppMetadata: appmetadata-<environment>-<client_id>

title: azr 0.3.5
text: CRAN release: 2026-06-26 Added azr_dataset(), azr_catalog(), azr_resolve_dataset() declaring resolving Azure Storage datasets across environment tiers. Added azr_logs_client() running KQL queries Azure Log Analytics REST API. Added ManagedIdentityCredential managed identity authentication via IMDS endpoint. Added azr_options(), option registry backing new azr.* options. Added azure_spark_storage_conf() generating Spark/Hadoop storage configuration keys, sovereign-cloud support. Added parse_storage_path() splitting Azure Storage URIs, including az:///azure:// schemes sovereign-cloud endpoints. api_client$new() gained verbose argument gates request response logging. azr_graph_client() gained endpoint argument Microsoft Graph host longer hardcoded. api_storage_client gained client_id argument, list_files() now pages results automatically. Breaking: get_credential_provider()’s interactive argument renamed allow_interactive, default_credential_chain() now tries workload identity, managed identity, Azure CLI ahead interactive credentials. base Credential class longer aborts construction non-interactive sessions (deferred get_token()), shared configuration checks moved private validate_base() helper. AzureCLICredential’s interactive argument renamed auto_login, login checked lazily get_token(). InteractiveCredential (AuthCodeCredential/DeviceCodeCredential) renamed interactive argument allow_prompt. cached_token_credential_chain() updated renamed allow_prompt/auto_login arguments. Internal: ClientSecretCredential’s $validate() now delegates shared validate_base() helper. Internal: RefreshTokenCredential’s $validate() now delegates shared validate_base() helper. WorkloadIdentityCredential now performs required tenant_id/client_id checks $validate() rather constructor body. default_azure_scope() now accepts short service names without azure_ prefix. Internal azure_services replaces azure_storage_endpoints/azure_scopes, consolidating per-service OAuth hosts data-plane endpoints. Added internal list_redact_pattern() redacting named list entries pattern. Added internal validate_required_string() deprecated_arg() helpers. Added .onLoad() hook registers S7 methods via S7::methods_register(). Added namespace imports S7 @ operator rlang’s :=.

title: azr 0.3.4
text: CRAN release: 2026-05-09 Removed set_azr_defaults() announced 0.3.3 never shipped. corresponding getOption() reads default_azure_host(), default_azure_client_id(), default_azure_tenant_id() also implemented; use environment variables (AZURE_AUTHORITY_HOST, AZURE_CLIENT_ID, AZURE_TENANT_ID) instead. WorkloadIdentityCredential now maintains -object token cache. Repeated calls get_token() return cached token immediately without re-reading federated token file exchanging , token expires. AZURE_AUTHORITY_HOST values https:// scheme prefix (recommended Azure SDK documentation) now handled correctly. Previously, setting AZURE_AUTHORITY_HOST=https://login.microsoftonline.com produce malformed token URLs (#16).

title: azr 0.3.3
text: CRAN release: 2026-05-04 Added set_azr_defaults() configure package-level overrides authority host, client ID, tenant ID. take priority environment variables default_azure_host(), default_azure_client_id(), default_azure_tenant_id(). AuthCodeCredential DeviceCodeCredential now maintain -object token cache keyed scope. Repeated calls get_token() scope return cached token immediately without hitting httr2 cache refresh token flow.

title: azr 0.3.2
text: CRAN release: 2026-03-30 is_hosted_session() now detects VS Code (VSCODE_INJECTION, VSCODE_PROXY_URI) Kubernetes (KUBERNETES_SERVICE_HOST) environments, supports azr.hosted option override. Extracted internal try_as_data_table() helper default_response_handler().

title: azr 0.3.1
text: CRAN release: 2026-03-04 Interactive credentials (AuthCodeCredential, DeviceCodeCredential) now attempt silent token acquisition via refresh token prompting user interactively.

title: azr 0.3.0
text: CRAN release: 2026-02-17 Added azr_storage_client interface Azure Storage API. Added CachedTokenCredential non-interactive sessions reuse previously cached tokens AuthCodeCredential DeviceCodeCredential without triggering new authentication flow. Refactored api_client$.fetch(): req_data now split separate query body arguments, req_method renamed method, headers argument added, path interpolation now uses rlang::englue() instead glue::glue().
